rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK (SOUTH DAKOTA) N.A. VS. CARLOS AYALA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, Line 2, Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Hearing required. There is no exemption for support on a bank levy. However, earnings that are determined to necessary for the support of the Judgment Debtor or his or her family are exempt under CCP § 706.051. If Judgment Debtor appears at the hearing and provides evidence showing that the levied funds are wages and necessary for his support, the claim will be granted. Judgment debtor should bring documents such as bank statements that show that the funds in the bank are wages, paychecks showing income, and bills showing the claimed expenses. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: EDDIE VALADEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Depo
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Friday, June 28, 2013, Line 2: Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Deposition Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. In advance of the hearing, the parties are to meet and confer to determine which, if any, items of discovery, responses to discovery, pleadings and other documents were served exclusively by electronic means. The parties will be asked to provide this information at the hearing. In addition, the Court did not receive a courtesy copy of the Declaration of Na'il Benjamin filed on June 14, although the face page of the pleading was received. Defendant is to provide a courtesy copy of the Benjamin declaration with all exhibits tabbed to the drop box outside of Department 301 no later than 4:30 p.m. on Thursday June 27 for the Court's review in advance of the hearing. The Court identifies the following motions as having been filed by the parties: (1) Motion to Quash the Subpoena to Jean Crossman-Miranda; (2) Motion for leave to take a second deposition of the plaintiff; (3) Motion to compel the deposition of Jean Crossman-Miranda; (4) Motion to compel plaintiff's further response to written discovery; (5) Motion to compel plaintiff to produce Dr. Everstine for deposition; (6) Motion to compel Dr. Wolf for deposition; and (7) Motion for sanctions against plaintiff. Each party shall bring to the hearing proposed orders as to each of these individual motions. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THOMPSON BELLINGRATH VS. FASTER LIGHTER BETTER, INC, DBA COPPER.IS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arb Adn Staying Action
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTs FASTER LIGHTER BETTER, INC, DBA COPPER.IS & SCOTT FRYXELL's Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration and Staying Action. Defendants' motion to compel arbitration is denied. Plaintiff's complaint is not within the scope of the parties' agreement to arbitrate. It alleges the violation of a different (and oral) agreement between the parties. The written consulting agreement does not have an integration clause or purport to supercede any earlier agreements. As a separate and alternative ground to deny the motion, Defendants waived their right to compel arbitration. The Complaint was filed on July 19, 2012. There is no affirmative defense demanding arbitration. Trial is set for July 15, 2013. This motion to compel arbitration was filed June 5, 2013, and was noticed for hearing a little more than 2 weeks before trial. Further to the prejudice of Plaintiff, Defendants have taken advantage of judicial discovery procedures not available in arbitration. See Saint Agnes Med. Ctr. v. PacifiCare of Calif. (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1203-1204. The courtesy copy of the Reply to Opposition to Motion for Order Compelling Arbitration and Staying Action does not have a page 3. This document is not scanned on the Court's register of actions. If the tentative ruling is contested, moving party is requested to bring a copy of that page of the reply brief to Department 302 tomorrow by 9:15 a.m., and, if possible, send an electronic copy of that page to the Court as soon as possible at contestdept302tr@sftc.org, clearly stating in the subject line the case name and number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT KENNETH PAGE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued on the Court's own motion to July 12, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MCALEESE, PAUL (DBA 530 JACKSON STREET, INC.) VS. RICHARD T. FERKO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANTs RICHARD FERKO & RICHARD T. FERKO, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Ferko's demurrer is sustained with 15 days leave to amend as to all causes of action to allege facts, if he can do so in good faith, that Paul McAleese is the real party in interest to sue on the claims alleged. The caption of the complaint is not sufficient in and of itself. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
